subject: english
topic_title: Speech Work: Introduction to Consonant Sounds
learning_objectives: Define consonant sounds and explain how they are produced
Identify all 24 consonant sounds of English using IPA symbols
Give examples of words containing each consonant sound

sections.heading: Definition
sections.content: Consonants are sounds realised when the air from the lungs is obstructed in some way. English has 24 consonant sounds.

sections.heading: The 24 Consonant Sounds
sections.content:
/m/ – voiced bilabial nasal: many, summer, bomb, damn
/p/ – voiceless bilabial plosive (upper and lower lips pressed together): picture, apple, tripper
/b/ – voiced bilabial plosive: baby, robber, baboon
/f/ – voiceless labio-dental fricative: firm, rough, physics
/v/ – voiced labio-dental fricative: vigour, Stephen, of, Volkswagen
/θ/ – voiceless dental fricative: thank, author, path
/ð/ – voiced dental fricative: than, that, gather
/n/ – voiced alveolar nasal: new, banner, pneumonia, known, gnash
/t/ – voiceless alveolar plosive: two, attack, Thomas, missed
/d/ – voiced alveolar plosive: standard, breed, sudden
/s/ – voiceless alveolar fricative: Saturday, miss, grass, rice, axe, science
/z/ – voiced alveolar fricative: zero, dizzy, please, scissors
/l/ – voiced alveolar lateral: lip, hello
/r/ – voiced alveolar approximant: rain, carrot, write, wrinkle, rhetoric
/ʃ/ – voiceless post-alveolar fricative: sure, schedule, mission, machine, nation
/ʒ/ – voiced post-alveolar fricative: measure, treasure, confusion, beige, Jean
/tʃ/ – voiceless post-alveolar affricate: cello, change, question, pasture
/dʒ/ – voiced post-alveolar affricate: jug, George, ginger, gym, educate
/j/ – voiceless palatal approximant: yam, hallelujah, unity, Europe
/ŋ/ – voiced velar nasal: sing, bank, anchor, include, anxious
/k/ – voiceless velar plosive: cat, crack, chemical, king, queue, account
/g/ – voiced velar plosive: goat, haggle, ghost
/w/ – voiced labio-velar approximant: wear, where, whistle
/h/ – voiceless glottal fricative: hope, whole, Llanfair
